item.title: U.S.-Iran peace efforts inch along, mired by Israel's fight with Hezbollah
item.description: A long-term U.S.-Iran peace deal may depend on a separate agreement between Israel and Lebanon. Analysts say that presents a problem.

item.title: Supreme Court upholds state transgender athlete bans in girls' and women's sports
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld state laws from West Virginia and Idaho that restricted participation by transgender athletes in girls' and women's sports.

item.title: Jill Schlesinger launches podcast to give financial tips and debunk bad advice
item.description: CBS News business analyst Jill Schlesinger is launching a new podcast called "Money Moves with Jill Schlesinger" to answer questions, offer advice and help you make the best financial decisions. She says they'll also challenge money myths you may hear online.

item.title: GOP Rep. Tom Kean returns to Congress after mysterious illness
item.description: The New Jersey congressman missed more than 140 votes since March 5 as those around him declined to give specifics about his medical issue.

item.title: Stunning upsets in World Cup
item.description: Paraguay knocked out historic powerhouse Germany in the World Cup on Monday – and it wasn't the only upset. Morocco beat the Netherlands in a penalty shootout, sending the Dutch to their earliest World Cup exit ever. Nicole Valdes reports.

item.title: AI could make people dull, one scientist fears. Here's why.
item.description: AI tends to "play it safe within a user's preferences," nudging people toward more conventional choices, according to computational social scientist Sandra Matz.

item.title: Behind the growing and controversial trend of looksmaxxing
item.description: Looksmaxxing is a viral and controversial trend that encourages young men to maximize their appearance. Adam Yamaguchi shows why it's resonating with some people.

item.title: Wildfires in Colorado rapidly spread, destroying buildings and forcing evacuations
item.description: New wildfires have quickly spread across tens of thousands of acres in Colorado, forcing people to evacuate from their homes and destroying buildings. Rob Marciano and Jonah Kaplan have the latest.

item.title: Rescuers cling to hope for survivors 6 days after Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: The earthquakes that hit Venezuela 6 days ago may have damaged or destroyed 58,000 buildings, NASA says, as rescuers race the clock to find survivors.

item.title: 3 advantages homebuyers should know this summer, according to experts
item.description: Even with mortgage interest rates high, buyers may have some timely advantages this summer. Here are three to know.

item.title: Colorado residents "might be coming home to nothing" as wildfires rage, evacuated woman says
item.description: High winds and low humidity are fueling raging wildfires in Colorado. Several firefighters have been killed amid efforts to contain the flames. CBS News correspondent Jonah Kaplan reports.

item.title: Inside the desperate search for trapped earthquake survivors in Venezuela
item.description: Time is running out for survivors trapped in rubble nearly a week after deadly earthquakes rocked Venezuela. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez spoke with families and rescue workers.

item.title: Ukrainian construction tycoon possibly targeted in Monaco explosion
item.description: French and Ukrainian media say an explosive device left in a Monaco apartment building seriously wounded construction magnate Vadym Iermolaiev and his wife.

item.title: JetBlue pilot says "we collided with a drone" while landing at JFK
item.description: The FAA is investigating after a JetBlue pilot said a drone "hit us right above the cockpit" while on its final approach to New York's JFK airport. JetBlue said a post-flight inspection found no signs of damage or any indication of a collision.

item.title: Maps show heat dome forecast to scorch major U.S. cities this week
item.description: A heat wave will blast much of the eastern U.S. this week, and forecasters say temperatures will feel even hotter because of the high humidity that's arriving with it.

item.title: Democratic primaries in Colorado mark latest fight over party's direction
item.description: One week after Democratic insurgent victories in New York, the focus is now moving to Colorado, where challenges in a trio of races are threatening candidates backed by the party establishment.

item.title: CBS Chicago journalists attacked by 3 men near Adler Planetarium
item.description: One of the men then smashed our photographer's camera while the other smashed the windshield of our news truck.

item.title: Colorado primaries test Democratic establishment amid progressive push
item.description: Colorado's Democratic Party could change following the results of Tuesday's primaries as younger progressives go up against established leaders in prominent races. CBS News' Taurean Small reports.

item.title: Dangerous heat spreads across the U.S.
item.description: Dozens of major U.S. cities are facing dangerous heat. It feels like more than 100 degrees from the Gulf to the Great Lakes. Rob Marciano has more.

item.title: NASA's unprecedented rescue mission to save aging telescope from falling to Earth
item.description: There's an urgent mission to save NASA's Swift Observatory, which is falling back into the Earth's atmosphere and at risk of burning up. Mark Strassmann reports on the rescue operation.

item.title: Ford recalls more than 741,000 vehicles due to faulty park system
item.description: Ford estimates that 1% of the vehicles have the defect, according to the recall notice.

item.title: Major Supreme Court decisions expected, including birthright citizenship
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday is expected to weigh in on the legality of President Trump's efforts to limit birthright citizenship, and several other big cases. Jan Crawford has more.

item.title: Recent alligator and crocodile attacks in Florida, Mexico raise safety concerns
item.description: There have been three alligator attacks in central Florida in a week, including an incident that killed a 31-year-old woman. Meanwhile in Mexico, a crocodile killed a 28-year-old tourist, authorities say. Cristian Benavides reports.

item.title: Over half a million dollars stolen from ATMs in "jackpotting" scheme
item.description: Four men are accused of stealing more than half a million dollars from ATMs in Connecticut, in a "jackpotting scheme," authorities said.

item.title: At least 3 injured, including Ukrainian businessman, in Monaco attack
item.description: Officials say a bomb went off at a residential building in Monaco, leaving at least three people injured, including a Ukrainian businessman. Chris Livesay reports.

item.title: Trump calls bipartisan housing bill "a yawn," insists on SAVE America Act passing
item.description: President Trump dismissed a bipartisan housing affordability bill passed by Congress as he pushed the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE America) Act. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi has more context.

item.title: Search for survivors of Venezuela earthquakes nears 1 week
item.description: More than 1,700 people have died in last week's twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela. Tens of thousands of people are still missing as rescuers continue to search through the rubble. Camilo Montoya-Galvez gives an inside look at rescue operations.

item.title: D.C.'s July 4th fireworks will have "TSA-style" security
item.description: This year's Fourth of July celebrations in D.C. — marking the nation's 250th birthday — will include hours of military flyovers and a massive fireworks display that could stretch late into the night.

item.title: This number helps explain why many Americans are down on the economy
item.description: American workers' share of the nation's income is at its lowest point in almost 80 years, as more of the economy's gains flow to corporations and investors.

item.title: CBS News poll on America at 250 — its successes and its challenges
item.description: A big majority say the nation has succeeded in achieving its founding ideals, at least a fair amount, if not a great deal. But Americans also see a nation facing challenges today, according to CBS News' most recent poll.
